author: Robert Carr
description: A skillet steak sandwich with thin-sliced seared steak, pepperoncini onions, garlic mayo, melted deli provolone, dressed arugula, and a toasted ciabatta or hoagie roll.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan

groceryIngredients.item: top sirloin steak, strip steak, or flat iron steak
ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll

ingredients: 6 to 8 ounces top sirloin steak, strip steak, or flat iron steak, ideally 1 to 1 1/4 inches thick
3/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1/2 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
1 teaspoon avocado oil or another neutral high-heat oil
1 (12-ounce) jar sliced pepperoncini peppers, using 2 tablespoons peppers and 1 teaspoon brine
1 small garlic clove, half finely grated and half smashed
1 ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll, split
1/2 ounce baby arugula, about 1/2 packed cup
instructions: Pat the steak very dry with paper towels.
Season both sides of the steak with 1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and 1/4 teaspoon smoked paprika.
Let the seasoned steak sit at room temperature for 15 to 20 minutes while preparing the sauce and onions.

Make the garlic mayo by stirring together 2 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/4 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 teaspoon pepperoncini brine, the finely grated garlic, and a pinch of black pepper in a small bowl.
Taste the garlic mayo and add only a few drops more pepperoncini brine if it needs more tang; keep it thick enough to cling to the roll.
Melt 1/2 tablespoon unsalted butter in a cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan over medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced yellow onion, a small pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a small pinch black pepper.
Cook the onion for 7 to 9 minutes, stirring every minute, until softened, sweet, and lightly browned at the edges.
Stir 2 tablespoons sliced pepperoncini peppers and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon pepperoncini brine into the onions.
Cook the pepperoncini onions for 1 to 2 minutes, until the brine has mostly evaporated and the onions taste bright and savory without being wet.
Transfer the pepperoncini onions to a plate.
Melt the remaining 1/2 tablespoon unsalted butter in the same skillet over medium heat.
Add the smashed garlic to the melted butter and swirl it for 20 to 30 seconds until fragrant.
Place the split ciabatta roll or hoagie roll cut-side down in the garlic butter and toast for 1 to 2 minutes, until the cut sides are golden.
Move the toasted roll to a plate and discard the smashed garlic.
Wipe out any loose crumbs and browned garlic bits from the skillet.
Increase the heat to medium-high, let the skillet heat for 1 minute, and add 1 teaspoon avocado oil.
Pat the steak dry again if moisture has appeared on the surface.
When the oil is shimmering, add the steak to the skillet.
Sear the steak for 3 to 4 minutes on the first side, without moving it, until well browned.
Flip the steak with tongs and sear the second side for 3 to 4 minutes, until the center reaches 120 to 125 degrees F for medium-rare after the final cheese melt or 130 degrees F for medium.
If the steak is browning faster than it is cooking through, reduce the heat to medium and keep cooking until it reaches the target temperature.
Transfer the steak to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 to 8 minutes.
Slice the rested steak as thinly as possible against the grain, angling the knife slightly for wider slices.
Toss the sliced steak with any cutting board juices and a tiny pinch of Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Return the sliced steak and any board juices to the skillet over low heat and gather it into 1 loose pile.
Top the steak pile with the pepperoncini onions and 1 slice deli provolone cheese.
Cover the skillet for 1 to 2 minutes, just until the provolone melts over the steak and onions.
While the cheese melts, toss 1/2 ounce baby arugula with 1/2 teaspoon fresh lemon juice, 1/4 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil, and a tiny pinch of Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on the toasted bottom roll.
Use tongs or a spatula to move the cheesy steak-and-onion pile onto the bottom roll.
Top the sandwich with the dressed arugula.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on the toasted top roll.
Close the sandwich and serve immediately while the roll is crisp and the cheese is melted.
notes: For speed, season the steak first, make the garlic mayo and onions while the salt starts working, then sear, slice, melt, and assemble.
For even better seasoning, salt the steak up to 24 hours ahead and refrigerate it uncovered on a plate or rack. Bring it out while preparing the sauce and onions.
Top sirloin works well here because it cooks quickly and slices well. Strip steak is richer; flat iron is very tender if sliced across the grain.
Avoid very thick steaks for this quick version. A 1 to 1 1/4 inch steak gives enough browning time without turning the recipe into a steakhouse project.
If using two smaller steak pieces, cook them one at a time. Crowding the skillet makes the steak steam instead of sear.
Pull the steak a few degrees below the final doneness you want because the sliced meat warms again while the cheese melts.
Use deli provolone for better melt and flavor. Provolone piccante is excellent here if you like a sharper cheese.
Use sliced pepperoncini peppers from a jar, not sweet banana peppers, because the brine adds acidity that cuts through the steak, mayo, butter, and cheese.
The garlic mayo should taste tangy, savory, and lightly sharp. If it tastes flat, add a few drops of pepperoncini brine before adding more salt.
Resting the steak for 5 to 8 minutes keeps the juices in the meat instead of soaking the bread.
Slice against the grain as thinly as possible. Thin slices make a quick steak sandwich feel tender without needing a marinade.
Dress the arugula right before assembly so it stays peppery and fresh instead of wilting.
Serve immediately. The best texture is crisp toasted bread, warm steak, melted cheese, bright onions, and fresh arugula all at once.
title: Garlic Steak Sandwich
